scope.summary: --allow-no-auth help and docs describe both stdio init-token disabling and HTTP unauthenticated local-development startup.
out_of_scope: No runtime auth behavior changed in this close; this bead is documentation/help-text alignment only.

entry_points.name: serve help text
entry_points.trace: crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs help says --allow-no-auth disables stdio's init-token requirement when no token is configured, permits HTTP to start without configured auth, and warns non-loopback HTTP still requires explicit remote opt-in.

entry_points.name: help text regression test
entry_points.trace: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target cargo test -p oraclemcp allow_no_auth_help_names_stdio_and_http_behavior -- --nocapture passed: 1 passed, 0 failed.

entry_points.name: documentation consistency
entry_points.trace: README.md, docs/configuration.md, and docs/behavior-inventory.md describe --allow-no-auth as explicit local-development auth bypass covering stdio and HTTP, with HTTP still subject to bind/auth guard posture.

limitations: Scoped local help-test proof only; no full-workspace clippy/test gate is claimed from this shared dirty checkout.
